openingMessage: We win in this business by being invited back the next day. That doesn't just come from doing the job right — it comes from how we carry ourselves as professionals and as people.
leadershipThought: We don't operate exposed. If there's an issue, call it in. Don't argue. Don't escalate. Execute.
opsContent:
Active Jobs (2026-06-29):
• Michael Hutt — SM Energy Coonskin Flowback: rig down by 0600 (4:24 AM notice); flow back done, rig down complete, waiting on orders (5:55 AM)
• Caleb Smith — Arrived at Shaw 183 (5:47 AM); also completed Shaw 13-8 #183, finished for the day (6:29 PM)
• Louie Zarate — Arrived at Dickenson-Jackson 19M 13H (6:05 AM); later finishing up at Meisser Raggett, new job called in, sent Blake the info, headed to Locklar Holiman 28N 114H (arrived 3:26 PM)
• Marylou Guerra — On location (6:13 AM)
• Jacob Hernandez — Arrived on Bizzel Irvin location, waiting on (6:13 AM+); job on Bizel Irvin begun (9:59 AM); job complete at Bizzel Irvin — conversion, atmospheric testing confirmed safe, below-grade conversion with Dvir arm utilized, Co Rep released, headed to shop (6:12 PM)
• Dawson Drake — Arrived at Schlitz A (6:51 AM); done at Schlitz, cleaned out two separators, SM HSE was continuously going around and was impressed with everything (3:30 PM)
• Gage Gonzales — Ovintiv HSC 320: currently pumping acid (6:54 AM); finished for the day, will resume ops 7:00 AM (2:23 PM)
• Jordan Lemmons — Arrived on location; MT and vac truck on site, no crew yet (7:18 AM)
• Ralph Forbes — Job finished, heading to shop (8:44 AM)
• Robert Wright — Job complete on the Merchant, heading to next location (9:54 AM)
• Stefanie Kirschke — Rig up completed on Venom (10:09 AM)
• Ray Solis — Done at DL Hutt, packing up equipment, heading to shop; reported something on fire off of 176 with photos (4:12 PM)
• Brianda Sandoval — Done at Schlitz Fed Com: completed two vessels, OL on LELs, everything else within acceptable standards, safety was around again (3:30 PM area)
• Marcos Morales — SM Energy/Coonskin/Flowback: rigged down with Michael Hutt, trailer set to side of location, headed back to mancamp

Carry-Forward:
• Jordan Lemmons: MT and vac truck on site, no crew present at 7:18 AM — job start status not confirmed
• Gage Gonzales: Ovintiv HSC 320 resuming ops 7:00 AM Tuesday
• Neck brace photo — identity and context unknown; needs follow-up
• Fire off HWY 176 — no crew impact reported, but worth noting if any crew was in that area
lessonsLearned:
• Rattlesnake on location — Ray Solis posted photos of a rattlesnake under/near equipment at 10:07 AM with caption "Be careful where you step." 🔴
• Something on fire off HWY 176 — Ray Solis reported and posted photos of smoke/fire visible from the road at 4:12 PM. Nature of fire not confirmed (grass fire likely). No crew involvement noted.
• Neck brace photo — Unidentified crew member photographed wearing a neck brace (1:34 PM). No context given in Breakroom. Flag for follow-up — unclear if work-related injury.
exxonMoments:
SWA Before Elevation — Unsafe Man Basket Stopped (Line of Fire)
A Canon technician at University 10-11/12H observed unsafe man basket operations and exercised Stop Work Authority before anyone was elevated. The location was left clean and safe after the situation was resolved. Man basket work is one of the highest-risk lift operations on an oil and gas site — SWA before elevation is always the right call when anything about the setup looks wrong.

Exclusion Zone Breach Intercepted — Third-Party Personnel (Line of Fire)
At Big Lake, a workover rig crew and third-party personnel attempted to enter established exclusion zones multiple times during a disconnect. The MT and roustabout crew actively intercepted unauthorized individuals and redirected them safely around the work area. When SimOps create unplanned traffic near your exclusion zone, proactive interception by all crew members — not just the safety tech — is what prevents a line-of-fire exposure.

Iron on the Ground, Not in the Air (Line of Fire)
At Guns Up O 32 29 #4215H, the crew laid iron on the ground instead of making changes to it while suspended. Job completed without incident. Keeping personnel out from under suspended loads is one of the most direct line-of-fire controls available — when you can set it down, set it down.

Spotter Lost Visual — Equipment Operator Called the Stop (Line of Fire)
A Sky Trax operator self-initiated a work stop the moment he lost visual contact with his spotter during operations. The crew identified better attention to wind direction as an area to improve. Equipment operators own Stop Work Authority — stop the moment you lose your spotter; regaining visual contact is the only acceptable restart condition.

Exclusion Zones Held — No Unauthorized Personnel On Pad (Line of Fire)
At TRD 006, the MT verified energy isolation while crane and manlift operated safely with exclusion zones effective throughout. No unauthorized personnel came onsite. Verified energy isolation combined with enforced exclusion zones is the complete line-of-fire control set — one without the other leaves a gap.

fieldNews.headline: Sunday Training — Confined Space Rescue + Davit Arm
fieldNews.detail: The team completed **Republic Safety LLC Confined Space Entry/Rescue Training** on Sunday June 28, including hands-on Davit arm training. Bobby Galindo shared Caleb Smith's completion certificate (instructor: Eddy Hada). This is the real work — showing up on a Sunday to build the skills that protect each other.

todaysChallenge: Find one person today and communicate something early — before they need it.
todaysChallengeSubtext: Call leadership. Notify dispatch. Post in Breakroom. No surprises. That is how trust is built.
